{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is whether avelumab is effective for bladder cancer. Reviewing the provided documents, Document [1] explicitly states that 'immune checkpoint inhibitors have shown activity in metastatic urothelial malignancies. These drugs reduce natural T-cell inhibition from their programmed death protein-1 (PD-1) cell surface protein, essentially releasing the T-cell's full immune potential. Examples include pembrolizumab, atezolizumab, avelumab, and nivolumab, which have all been approved for use in bladder cancer patients. Avelumab and pembrolizumab have shown improved overall survival in clinical trials.' This directly indicates that avelumab has been approved for use in bladder cancer and has demonstrated improved overall survival in clinical trials, confirming its effectiveness. The UMLS documents do not provide relevant information about avelumab's effectiveness and instead discuss alirocumab and its associations with cardiac muscle formation and bladder cancer, which is not pertinent to the query. Therefore, based on the MEDCORP documents, avelumab is effective for bladder cancer.", "answer_choice": "A"}
